cases.description: Excel/LO ROUND uses arithmetic rounding (half away from zero), NOT banker's rounding (round-half-to-even)
cases.expected_note: Banker's rounding would give 2; correct spreadsheet behavior is 3

cases.description: Negative half-values also round away from zero
Negative num_digits rounds to the left of the decimal point

Tests whether the engine falls into the naive-binary-float trap: the IEEE-754 double closest to 1.005 is actually ~1.00499999999999989, so a naive floor(x*100+0.5)/100 implementation would round DOWN to 1.00
cases.expected_note: Both Excel and LibreOffice correctly return 1.01, not 1.0 -- they normalize to ~15 significant decimal digits before rounding, specifically to avoid this well-known binary-float pitfall
